headline: The Ceasefire Broke at Sea
dek: Nine consecutive nights of renewed U.S. strikes, fresh damage to merchant vessels and thousands of stranded seafarers show why the Strait of Hormuz is now both a battlefield and a test of the maritime order.
author: APGO World Desk
imageAlt: Commercial ships moving through a narrow, hazy maritime channel.
imageCaption: Conceptual editorial visual; not a documentary photograph.

headline: Ankara’s Long-War Commitment
dek: NATO has turned support for Ukraine into a two-year industrial pledge while expanding its eastern defences. The strategy is becoming more durable just as UN monitors record the worst monthly civilian toll since spring 2022.
author: APGO World Desk
imageAlt: Freight rail infrastructure and tarped logistics equipment at dawn.
imageCaption: Conceptual editorial visual; not a documentary photograph.

headline: The Debt Problem Is Now a Calendar
dek: The world’s borrowing debate usually begins with how much is owed. In 2026, the sharper risk is when that debt matures, what rate replaces it and how much public policy gets crowded out during the rollover.
author: APGO Economics Desk
imageAlt: A financial operations desk arranged around a stack of blank maturity cards.

headline: The AI Boom Has Reached the Substation
dek: Data-center demand is growing faster than the power system built to serve it. The decisive questions are no longer confined to chips and models: they are about transformers, transmission, credible forecasts and who pays when a promised load fails to arrive.
author: APGO Technology Desk
imageAlt: A data-center complex beside electrical substation equipment at blue hour.

headline: The Declaration Was a Distribution System Before It Was a Sacred Object
dek: Two hundred and fifty years after independence, the familiar parchment can obscure the more radical story: a text set in type overnight, carried by riders, reprinted by newspapers and made political by being read aloud.
author: APGO History Desk
imageAlt: A museum conservation table with broadside-sized paper, gloves and a magnifier.
imageCaption: Conceptual editorial visual; no pictured document is presented as an original.

headline: The Sun Also Rises Turned Exile Into an Export
dek: A century after publication, Hemingway’s first major novel still shapes how outsiders imagine Paris, Pamplona and expatriate life—even as the book itself is more wounded, exclusionary and skeptical than the lifestyle myth built around it.
author: APGO Books Desk
imageAlt: A worn book, travel notebook and café glass on a contemporary desk.

sources.name: John F. Kennedy Presidential Library — Hemingway’s works by title and publication chronology

headline: One Goal, Two Stars: Spain Outlast Argentina to Reclaim the World Cup
dek: Ferran Torres’s 106th-minute finish settled a final built on Spanish pressure and Argentine resistance. The 1–0 victory gave Spain a second men’s title—and completed an unusually concentrated run of international success.
author: APGO Football Desk
imageAlt: A floodlit football pitch and stadium tunnel after a night match.
imageCaption: Conceptual editorial visual; not a photograph of the final.

headline: The 48-Team World Cup Is Over. Its Scale Was the Story.
dek: More countries reached the stage, more people entered the grounds and the scoring rate rose. The first expanded World Cup answered the simplest sporting objection—but left harder questions about format, travel and workload for a calmer audit.
author: APGO Football Desk
imageAlt: A vast stadium bowl and its layered concourses seen in daylight.
imageCaption: Conceptual editorial visual; not a photograph from the tournament.
